description: Main biological definitions and formulas that are not part of the units, physics, math or chemistry base data.

words.name: biology
words.description: the natural science that studies life and living organisms

words.name: genetics
words.description: the branch of biology that studies genes, heredity and variation

words.name: ecology
words.description: the branch of biology that studies organisms and their relationship with the environment

words.name: physiology
words.description: the branch of biology that studies the functions and mechanisms of living systems

words.name: microbiology
words.description: the branch of biology that studies microscopic organisms

words.name: cell
words.description: the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms

words.name: cytoplasm
words.description: the material within a cell, excluding the nucleus

words.name: organelle
words.description: a specialized subunit within a cell that has a specific function

words.name: mitochondrion
words.description: the organelle that produces most of the cell supply of ATP

words.name: chloroplast
words.description: the organelle that conducts photosynthesis in plant cells

words.name: ribosome
words.description: the cellular structure that synthesizes proteins

words.name: cytoskeleton
words.description: the network of filaments that gives a cell its shape and internal organization

words.name: mitosis
words.description: cell division producing two genetically identical daughter cells

words.name: meiosis
words.description: cell division producing four genetically diverse gametes

words.name: prokaryote
words.description: a single-celled organism that lacks a membrane-bound nucleus

words.name: eukaryote
words.description: an organism whose cells contain a membrane-bound nucleus

words.name: DNA
words.description: the molecule that carries the genetic instructions of an organism

words.name: RNA
words.description: the molecule that conveys genetic information from DNA to ribosomes

words.name: gene
words.description: a unit of heredity that is a segment of DNA coding for a function

words.name: genome
words.description: the complete set of genetic material of an organism

words.name: chromosome
words.description: a threadlike structure of DNA carrying genes

words.name: nucleotide
words.description: the building block of nucleic acids such as DNA and RNA

words.name: codon
words.description: a sequence of three nucleotides that codes for an amino acid

words.name: transcription
words.description: the process of copying a DNA sequence into messenger RNA

words.name: translation
words.description: the process of synthesizing a protein from a messenger RNA template

words.name: protein
words.description: a large biomolecule made of chains of amino acids

words.name: heredity
words.description: the passing of traits from parents to offspring

words.name: genotype
words.description: the genetic constitution of an organism

words.name: phenotype
words.description: the observable characteristics of an organism

words.name: allele
words.description: one of two or more alternative forms of a gene

words.name: homozygous
words.description: having two identical alleles of a gene

words.name: heterozygous
words.description: having two different alleles of a gene

words.name: trait
words.description: a distinct variant of a characteristic of an organism

words.name: mutation
words.description: a change in the nucleotide sequence of the genome

words.name: evolution
words.description: the change in heritable characteristics of populations over generations

words.name: adaptation
words.description: a trait that increases the fitness of an organism in its environment

words.name: species
words.description: a group of organisms capable of interbreeding and producing fertile offspring

words.name: speciation
words.description: the evolutionary process by which new species arise

words.name: fitness
words.description: the reproductive success of an organism in a given environment

words.name: phylogeny
words.description: the evolutionary history and relationships of a group of organisms

words.name: biodiversity
words.description: the variety of life in a habitat or ecosystem

words.name: ecosystem
words.description: a community of organisms together with their physical environment

words.name: population
words.description: all the individuals of a species living in a given area

words.name: community
words.description: all the populations of different species in a given area

words.name: habitat
words.description: the natural environment in which an organism lives

words.name: producer
words.description: an organism that makes its own food, usually by photosynthesis

words.name: consumer
words.description: an organism that obtains energy by feeding on other organisms

words.name: decomposer
words.description: an organism that breaks down dead matter into simpler substances

words.name: biome
words.description: a large community of plants and animals occupying a major habitat

words.name: symbiosis
words.description: a close and long-term biological interaction between two species

words.name: organ
words.description: a group of tissues that perform a specific function

words.name: tissue
words.description: a group of similar cells performing a specific function

words.name: homeostasis
words.description: the maintenance of a stable internal environment

words.name: hormone
words.description: a signaling molecule that regulates physiology and behavior

words.name: neuron
words.description: an electrically excitable cell that transmits nerve impulses

words.name: photosynthesis
words.description: the process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy

words.name: fermentation
words.description: the anaerobic breakdown of glucose to release energy

words.name: magnification
words.description: the ratio of the apparent size of an image to the real size of the object

words.name: microorganism
words.description: a microscopic organism such as a bacterium or virus

words.name: bacterium
words.description: a single-celled prokaryotic microorganism

words.name: virus
words.description: an infectious agent that replicates only inside living cells

words.name: pathogen
words.description: an organism that causes disease

words.name: antibiotic
words.description: a substance that kills or inhibits the growth of bacteria

words.name: antibody
words.description: a protein produced by the immune system to neutralize pathogens

words.name: antigen
words.description: a molecule that triggers an immune response

triples.name: cell biology
triples.description: the branch of biology that studies the structure and function of cells

triples.name: molecular biology
triples.description: the branch of biology that studies biological activity at the molecular level

triples.name: evolutionary biology
triples.description: the branch of biology that studies how populations of organisms change over generations

triples.name: cell membrane
triples.description: the semipermeable membrane that surrounds the cytoplasm of a cell

triples.name: cell nucleus
triples.description: the membrane-bound organelle that contains the cell genetic material

triples.name: cell wall
triples.description: the rigid layer outside the cell membrane in plants, fungi and bacteria

triples.name: cell division
triples.description: the process by which a parent cell divides into daughter cells

triples.name: cell cycle
triples.description: the series of events that lead to cell division and duplication

triples.name: stem cell
triples.description: an undifferentiated cell that can differentiate into specialized cells

triples.name: base pair
triples.description: two complementary nucleobases bonded across the DNA double helix

triples.name: gene expression
triples.description: the process by which information from a gene is used to make a product

triples.name: amino acid
triples.description: an organic molecule that is the building block of proteins

triples.name: nucleic acid
triples.description: a biopolymer such as DNA or RNA built from nucleotides

triples.name: dominant allele
triples.description: an allele that masks the effect of a recessive allele

triples.name: recessive allele
triples.description: an allele whose effect is masked by a dominant allele

triples.name: genetic code
triples.description: the set of rules by which codons are translated into amino acids

triples.name: allele frequency
triples.description: the relative frequency of an allele in a population

triples.name: genotype frequency
triples.description: the relative frequency of a genotype in a population

triples.name: gene pool
triples.description: the complete set of genes in a population

triples.name: Mendelian inheritance
triples.description: inheritance following the laws formulated by Gregor Mendel

triples.name: Hardy-Weinberg principle
triples.description: the principle that allele and genotype frequencies stay constant without evolutionary influences

triples.name: natural selection
triples.description: the differential survival and reproduction of individuals due to traits

triples.name: common ancestor
triples.description: an ancestral organism shared by two or more descendant groups

triples.name: genetic drift
triples.description: the change in allele frequencies due to random chance

triples.name: ecological niche
triples.description: the role and position a species has in its environment

triples.name: food chain
triples.description: a linear sequence of organisms through which energy and nutrients pass

triples.name: food web
triples.description: a network of interconnected food chains in an ecosystem

triples.name: trophic level
triples.description: a position in a food chain defined by feeding relationships

triples.name: carrying capacity
triples.description: the maximum population size an environment can sustain

triples.name: gross primary production
triples.description: the total amount of energy fixed by producers in an ecosystem

triples.name: net primary production
triples.description: the energy fixed by producers minus their respiration

triples.name: cellular respiration
triples.description: the process by which cells release energy from glucose

triples.name: heart rate
triples.description: the number of heartbeats per unit of time

triples.name: stroke volume
triples.description: the volume of blood pumped by the heart in one beat

triples.name: cardiac output
triples.description: the volume of blood the heart pumps per minute

triples.name: body mass index
triples.description: a measure of body fat based on mass and height

triples.name: birth rate
triples.description: the number of births per unit of population and time

triples.name: death rate
triples.description: the number of deaths per unit of population and time

triples.name: population growth rate
triples.description: the change in population size per unit of time

triples.name: image size
triples.description: the measured size of a magnified image

triples.name: object size
triples.description: the real size of an observed object

triples.name: normal human body temperature
triples.description: the typical core body temperature of a healthy human, about 37 degree Celsius

triples.name: human chromosome number
triples.description: the number of chromosomes in a typical human somatic cell
